Photosynthesis is the process where plants convert light energy, usually from the sun, into chemical energy in the form of glucose (sugar). This occurs primarily in the leaves and involves taking in carbon dioxide ($\text{CO}_2$) from the air and water ($\text{H}_2\text{O}$) from the soil. The process uses chlorophyll to capture light energy, which is then used to convert $\text{CO}_2$ and $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ into glucose and oxygen ($\text{O}_2$) as a byproduct.

TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) and UDP (User Datagram Protocol) are both transport layer protocols used for sending data over the internet, but they differ primarily in their connection-oriented nature, reliability, and speed.

| Feature | TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) | UDP (User Datagram Protocol) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Connection** | Connection-oriented (requires a handshake before data transfer). | Connectionless (sends data without prior setup). |

**Opportunity cost** is the value of the next best alternative that must be forgone when making a choice.

**Simple Example:** If you have $\$10$ and can either buy a book or a movie ticket, and you choose the book, the opportunity cost is the enjoyment or experience you would have gotten from the movie ticket.

The Earth has seasons primarily because of its **axial tilt** of approximately 23.5 degrees relative to its orbit around the Sun. This tilt causes different parts of the Earth to receive varying amounts of direct solar energy throughout the year, leading to different seasons.
